page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 4 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation, 4 physical cores, and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 33 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, and FMA. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps under the KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations position the ecs.n4.xlarge in the lower performance tiers. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, including stress-ng and Geekbench, consistently rank in the bottom 25% or bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth for large-block writes is average, but read-only bandwidth and Passmark memory scores fall into the bottom 10% to 25% categories. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3 operations, also show poor performance.

Given its shared CPU allocation and modest performance profile, this instance is structured as a budget-friendly option for non-critical workloads. It is not suited for resource-intensive database operations or heavy computational tasks. Instead, the ecs.n4.xlarge is best utilized for lightweight applications, basic web serving, development environments, or low-traffic background processes where cost minimization is prioritized over high-throughput performance.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 4 vCPUs on a shared Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating under the KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates limited performance, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics, memory read bandwidth, and compression speeds largely falling into the bottom 10% to 25% cohorts. Consequently, this instance represents a budget-oriented tier with low resource density. It is best suited for lightweight workloads, such as basic web serving, development testing, and low-intensity background tasks where high compute performance is not required.

8.0 GB of system memory with no local storage or GPU hardware
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth under KVM virtualization
